instructions.tips: Press breadcrumbs firmly for good adhesion!

instructions.title: Heat butter
instructions.instruction: Heat clarified butter in large pan over medium-high heat until shimmering but not smoking. Butter should be deep enough to come halfway up cutlets.
instructions.tips: Proper temperature is crucial!

instructions.title: Fry cutlets
instructions.instruction: Fry cutlets one or two at a time 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. Don't move them until ready to flip! Drain on paper towels.
instructions.tips: Patience = perfect crust!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Serve immediately with lemon wedges and parsley. Traditionally served with potato salad and cranberry sauce.
instructions.tips: Squeeze lemon over just before eating!

storage.fridge: Best eaten fresh; not recommended for storage
storage.reheating: Oven at 375°F for 10 minutes to re-crisp

recipes.name: Swedish Meatballs
recipes.description: Tender meatballs in creamy gravy with lingonberry sauce

instructions.title: Make meatball mixture
instructions.instruction: Soak breadcrumbs in milk 5 minutes. In bowl, mix beef, pork, soaked breadcrumbs, onion, egg, allspice, nutmeg, salt, and pepper until just combined. Don't overmix!
instructions.tips: Overmixing = tough meatballs!

instructions.title: Form meatballs
instructions.instruction: With wet hands, roll mixture into 1-inch balls (about 24 meatballs). Place on plate.
instructions.tips: Wet hands prevent sticking!

instructions.title: Cook meatballs
instructions.instruction: Melt 2 tbsp butter in large pan over medium heat. Cook meatballs in batches, turning frequently, until browned all over and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes per batch. Remove and set aside.
instructions.tips: Don't crowd the pan!

instructions.title: Make gravy
instructions.instruction: In same pan, melt remaining 2 tbsp butter. Add flour, whisk 2 minutes until golden. Gradually add beef stock, whisking constantly. Bring to simmer.
instructions.tips: Whisk constantly to prevent lumps!

instructions.title: Finish gravy
instructions.instruction: Stir in cream and soy sauce. Simmer 5 minutes until thickened. Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
instructions.tips: Soy sauce adds umami depth!

instructions.title: Combine and serve
instructions.instruction: Return meatballs to gravy. Simmer 5 minutes. Garnish with parsley. Serve with mashed potatoes and lingonberry jam.
instructions.tips: Lingonberries are traditional!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat, add splash of stock if needed

recipes.name: Ratatouille
recipes.description: Provençal vegetable stew with eggplant, zucchini, and tomatoes

instructions.title: Salt eggplant
instructions.instruction: Sprinkle diced eggplant with salt, let sit 15 minutes. Rinse and pat dry.
instructions.tips: Removes bitterness and excess moisture!

instructions.title: Cook onions
instructions.instruction: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in Dutch oven over medium heat. Cook onion 8 minutes until soft and translucent.
instructions.tips: Slow-cooked onions build sweet base!

instructions.title: Add garlic and tomato paste
instructions.instruction: Add garlic and tomato paste, cook 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
instructions.tips: Cooking tomato paste removes raw taste!

instructions.title: Cook eggplant
instructions.instruction: Add remaining olive oil and eggplant. Cook 8 minutes until beginning to soften.
instructions.tips: Eggplant needs more oil to cook properly!

instructions.title: Add remaining vegetables
instructions.instruction: Add zucchini and bell peppers. Cook 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
instructions.tips: Layer vegetables for best texture!

instructions.title: Add tomatoes and herbs
instructions.instruction: Add chopped tomatoes, thyme, bay leaf, salt, and pepper. Reduce heat to low, cover. Simmer 25-30 minutes until all vegetables are tender and flavors have melded.
instructions.tips: Low simmer develops rich flavors!

instructions.title: Finish and serve
instructions.instruction: Remove thyme sprigs and bay leaf. Stir in fresh basil. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve hot or at room temperature with crusty bread.
instructions.tips: Tastes even better the next day!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat or microwave 3-4 minutes

recipes.name: Mole Poblano
recipes.description: Rich chocolate-chili sauce with chicken and complex spices

instructions.title: Cook chicken
instructions.instruction: Place chicken in pot with 2 cups stock and 1 tsp salt. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook 30 minutes until tender. Remove chicken, reserve stock.
instructions.tips: Keep chicken warm while making sauce!

instructions.title: Toast chilies
instructions.instruction: Heat dry pan over medium heat. Toast chilies 30 seconds per side until fragrant but not burnt. Place in bowl, cover with hot water, soak 20 minutes. Drain.
instructions.tips: Don't burn chilies or mole will be bitter!

instructions.title: Char vegetables
instructions.instruction: In same dry pan, char tomatoes, onion, and garlic until blackened in spots, about 8 minutes total. Set aside.
instructions.tips: Charring adds smoky depth!

instructions.title: Toast spices and nuts
instructions.instruction: Toast sesame seeds, almonds, cinnamon, cloves, peppercorns, cumin, and coriander in dry pan 3-4 minutes until fragrant. Set aside.
instructions.tips: Toasting releases essential oils!

instructions.title: Fry tortilla
instructions.instruction: Heat 1 tbsp lard in pan. Fry tortilla pieces until crispy, about 2 minutes. Set aside.
instructions.tips: Tortilla helps thicken the sauce!

instructions.title: Blend sauce
instructions.instruction: In blender, combine soaked chilies, charred vegetables, toasted spices and nuts, fried tortilla, raisins, and 1 cup reserved chicken stock. Blend until very smooth.
instructions.tips: Blend in batches if needed!

instructions.title: Cook mole
instructions.instruction: Heat remaining 2 tbsp lard in large pot over medium heat. Pour in blended sauce (it will splatter!). Cook 15 minutes, stirring constantly. Add remaining stock gradually. Simmer 20 minutes.
instructions.tips: Constant stirring prevents burning!

instructions.title: Finish sauce
instructions.instruction: Stir in chocolate, sugar, and remaining salt. Simmer 10 minutes until chocolate melts and sauce thickens. Taste and adjust seasoning.
instructions.tips: Mole should coat the back of a spoon!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Add chicken to sauce, coat well. Serve with rice, beans, and warm tortillas. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
instructions.tips: Mole tastes even better the next day!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat, add splash of stock if needed

recipes.name: Feijoada
recipes.description: Black bean stew with pork and beef served with rice and farofa

instructions.title: Start beans
instructions.instruction: Drain soaked beans. Place in large pot with 8 cups water, bay leaves, and orange quarters. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook 60 minutes.
instructions.tips: Orange adds subtle brightness!

instructions.title: Brown meats
instructions.instruction: While beans cook, heat olive oil in pan. Season all meats with salt and pepper. Brown pork shoulder, short ribs, and pork ribs in batches, about 10 minutes total.
instructions.tips: Browning builds flavor!

instructions.title: Add meats to beans
instructions.instruction: After beans have cooked 60 minutes, add browned meats to pot. Continue simmering 60 minutes.
instructions.tips: Low simmer keeps beans intact!

instructions.title: Cook bacon and aromatics
instructions.instruction: In same pan, cook bacon until crispy, 6-7 minutes. Add onions, cook 5 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
instructions.tips: Use bacon fat for flavor!

instructions.title: Add to stew
instructions.instruction: Add bacon mixture and sliced sausage to pot. Simmer another 30 minutes until meats are very tender and beans are creamy.
instructions.tips: Beans should be soft and creamy!

instructions.title: Thicken stew
instructions.instruction: Remove 2 cups beans with some liquid. Mash and return to pot to thicken stew. Remove orange quarters and bay leaves. Taste and adjust salt.
instructions.tips: Mashed beans create creamy texture!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Serve in bowls over white rice. Garnish with green onions and orange slices. Traditionally served with farofa, collard greens, and hot sauce.
instructions.tips: A feast for special occasions!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat, add water if needed

recipes.name: Peruvian Ceviche
recipes.description: Fresh fish marinated in citrus with onions and chili peppers

ingredients.name: white fish
ingredients.preparation: very fresh, sea bass or halibut, cut into 1/2-inch cubes

instructions.title: Prepare onions
instructions.instruction: Soak sliced red onions in ice water with pinch of salt for 10 minutes. Drain and pat dry. This removes harshness.
instructions.tips: This step is crucial for sweet onions!

instructions.title: Make leche de tigre
instructions.instruction: In blender, combine 1/4 cup lime juice, garlic, ginger, aji amarillo, salt, pepper, and ice cubes. Blend until frothy. This is the marinade base.
instructions.tips: Ice keeps everything super cold!

instructions.title: Marinate fish
instructions.instruction: Place fish cubes in glass bowl. Pour leche de tigre and remaining lime juice over fish. Add half the cilantro. Gently toss. Marinate in fridge 10-15 minutes only! Fish should be opaque but still tender.
instructions.tips: Don't over-marinate or fish gets mushy!

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.instruction: Add drained onions to fish mixture. Toss gently.
instructions.tips: Mix just before serving!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Divide ceviche among plates. Garnish with remaining cilantro. Serve with boiled sweet potato rounds and corn. Spoon extra leche de tigre over top. Serve immediately!
instructions.tips: Must be served ice cold and fresh!

storage.reheating: N/A - served cold

recipes.name: Argentine Empanadas
recipes.description: Savory pastries filled with spiced beef, olives, and hard-boiled eggs

ingredients.name: eggs
ingredients.preparation: one for dough, one beaten for wash

instructions.title: Make dough
instructions.instruction: Mix flour and 1 tsp salt in bowl. Cut in cold butter until crumbly. Add 1 egg and warm water, mix until dough forms. Knead briefly. Wrap in plastic, refrigerate 30 minutes.
instructions.tips: Cold butter = flaky pastry!

instructions.title: Make filling
instructions.instruction: Heat oil in pan over medium-high heat. Cook onions and bell pepper 6 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute. Add beef, cook 8 minutes until browned. Season with cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
instructions.tips: Cook beef until no pink remains!

instructions.title: Finish filling
instructions.instruction: Remove from heat. Stir in olives, raisins, and chopped eggs. Let cool completely.
instructions.tips: Must be cold before filling empanadas!

instructions.title: Roll and cut dough
instructions.instruction: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Roll dough to 1/8-inch thick. Cut into 5-inch circles using bowl or cutter. Re-roll scraps.
instructions.tips: Keep dough cold while working!

instructions.title: Fill empanadas
instructions.instruction: Place 2 tbsp filling on one half of each circle. Brush edges with beaten egg. Fold over, press edges to seal. Crimp with fork or do repulgue (traditional braid).
instructions.tips: Make sure edges are well sealed!

instructions.title: Bake
instructions.instruction: Place empanadas on parchment-lined baking sheet. Brush tops with beaten egg. Bake 22-25 minutes until golden brown.
instructions.tips: Egg wash gives beautiful shine!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Let cool 5 minutes before serving. Serve with chimichurri sauce.
instructions.tips: Great hot or at room temperature!

storage.freezer: Unbaked empanadas freeze up to 3 months
storage.reheating: Oven at 350°F for 10-12 minutes

recipes.name: Cuban Ropa Vieja
recipes.description: Shredded beef in tomato sauce with peppers and onions

instructions.title: Brown beef
instructions.instruction: Season flank steak with salt and pepper. Heat 2 tbsp oil in Dutch oven over high heat. Brown beef 3-4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
instructions.tips: Good sear = more flavor!

instructions.title: Cook beef
instructions.instruction: Return beef to pot. Add beef stock and bay leaves. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cover and cook 90 minutes until very tender and easy to shred.
instructions.tips: Low simmer = tender meat!

instructions.title: Shred beef
instructions.instruction: Remove beef to cutting board. Let cool slightly. Shred meat with two forks into thin strips. Reserve cooking liquid.
instructions.tips: Shred along the grain for best texture!

instructions.title: Make sauce
instructions.instruction: Heat remaining 1 tbsp oil in same pot over medium heat. Cook bell peppers and onion 8 minutes until soft. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
instructions.tips: Soften vegetables well!

instructions.title: Add tomatoes and spices
instructions.instruction: Add crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, cumin, oregano. Cook 5 minutes, stirring.
instructions.tips: Let tomato paste cook out!

instructions.title: Braise beef in sauce
instructions.instruction: Add wine, 1 cup reserved cooking liquid, shredded beef, olives, and capers. Simmer uncovered 30 minutes until sauce thickens and flavors meld.
instructions.tips: Sauce should be rich and coat meat!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Remove bay leaves. Taste and adjust salt. Garnish with cilantro. Serve over white rice with black beans and fried plantains.
instructions.tips: Classic Cuban comfort food!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat or microwave 3-4 minutes

recipes.name: Chilean Pastel de Choclo
recipes.description: Corn casserole with ground beef, chicken, olives, and hard-boiled eggs

instructions.title: Make beef filling
instructions.instruction: Heat oil in pan over medium-high heat. Cook onions 6 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute. Add beef, cook 8 minutes until browned. Season with cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Stir in raisins. Set aside.
instructions.tips: Raisins add sweet contrast!

instructions.title: Make corn topping
instructions.instruction: In blender, combine corn kernels, milk, butter, basil, sugar, and pinch of salt. Blend until smooth but still slightly chunky.
instructions.tips: Don't over-blend - some texture is good!

instructions.title: Cook corn mixture
instructions.instruction: Pour corn mixture into pot over medium heat. Cook 10-12 minutes, stirring constantly, until thickened to soft polenta consistency.
instructions.tips: Constant stirring prevents sticking!

instructions.title: Assemble casserole
instructions.instruction: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Spread beef mixture in bottom of greased 9x13 baking dish. Top with shredded chicken. Arrange egg halves, olives scattered throughout.
instructions.tips: Even layers cook evenly!

instructions.title: Add corn topping
instructions.instruction: Spread corn mixture over top, covering completely. Smooth surface. Sprinkle with a little extra sugar if desired.
instructions.tips: Sugar on top caramelizes beautifully!

instructions.title: Bake
instructions.instruction: Bake 30-35 minutes until top is golden and slightly caramelized. Let rest 10 minutes before serving.
instructions.tips: Resting makes it easier to slice!

storage.reheating: Oven at 350°F for 20 minutes

recipes.name: Colombian Ajiaco
recipes.description: Chicken and potato soup with corn and Colombian herbs

instructions.title: Cook chicken
instructions.instruction: Place chicken breasts in large pot with chicken stock, half the green onions, garlic, salt, and pepper. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook 25 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
instructions.tips: Don't overcook chicken!

instructions.title: Shred chicken
instructions.instruction: Remove chicken to plate. When cool enough to handle, remove bones and shred meat. Set aside. Strain broth.
instructions.tips: Reserve broth - it's flavorful!

instructions.title: Cook potatoes
instructions.instruction: Return broth to pot. Add all three types of potatoes. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook 20 minutes until russet potatoes start to break down and thicken soup.
instructions.tips: Different potatoes create creamy texture!

instructions.title: Add corn and herbs
instructions.instruction: Add corn pieces and guascas. Simmer 10 minutes.
instructions.tips: Guascas is the authentic Colombian flavor!

instructions.title: Finish soup
instructions.instruction: Return shredded chicken to pot. Add remaining green onions and cilantro. Simmer 5 minutes. Taste and adjust salt.
instructions.tips: Soup should be creamy from broken-down potatoes!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Ladle soup into bowls with corn pieces. Serve with small bowls of cream, capers, and avocado slices on the side for everyone to add as desired.
instructions.tips: Toppings are essential to authentic ajiaco!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over medium heat until hot

recipes.name: Ecuadorian Encebollado
recipes.description: Fish soup with yuca, pickled onions, and citrus

instructions.title: Make pickled onions
instructions.instruction: Mix thinly sliced red onions with half the lime juice and pinch of salt. Set aside to pickle while cooking soup.
instructions.tips: These add brightness to the soup!

instructions.title: Cook aromatics
instructions.instruction: Heat oil in large pot over medium heat. Cook diced onion 6 minutes. Add garlic, tomatoes, cumin, and aji amarillo. Cook 5 minutes until tomatoes break down.
instructions.tips: Build flavor base!

instructions.title: Add yuca
instructions.instruction: Add fish stock and yuca chunks. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook 25 minutes until yuca is very tender.
instructions.tips: Yuca thickens the broth!

instructions.title: Add fish
instructions.instruction: Season tuna with salt and pepper. Add to pot. Simmer gently 8-10 minutes until fish is just cooked through. Don't overcook!
instructions.tips: Gentle simmer keeps fish tender!

instructions.title: Finish soup
instructions.instruction: Stir in remaining lime juice, orange juice, and half the cilantro. Taste and adjust salt.
instructions.tips: Citrus brightens everything!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Ladle soup into bowls. Top with pickled onions, remaining cilantro, and popcorn. Serve with extra lime wedges.
instructions.tips: Popcorn on soup is traditional!

storage.reheating: Gentle heat on stovetop

recipes.name: Venezuelan Pabellón Criollo
recipes.description: Shredded beef with black beans, rice, and fried plantains

instructions.title: Cook beef
instructions.instruction: Place flank steak in Dutch oven with beef stock, 1 bay leaf, half the garlic, salt, and pepper. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cover and cook 90 minutes until very tender. Remove beef, reserve 2 cups cooking liquid. Shred beef with forks.
instructions.tips: Save that flavorful cooking liquid!

instructions.title: Cook beans
instructions.instruction: While beef cooks, drain soaked beans. Place in pot with 6 cups water and remaining bay leaf. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook 60 minutes until tender. Drain and season with salt.
instructions.tips: Don't add salt until beans are tender!

instructions.title: Make shredded beef sauce
instructions.instruction: Heat 2 tbsp oil in pan over medium heat. Cook onion and bell pepper 6 minutes. Add remaining garlic, cook 1 minute. Add tomatoes, cumin, paprika. Cook 5 minutes. Add shredded beef and 2 cups reserved cooking liquid. Simmer 20 minutes until sauce thickens.
instructions.tips: Sauce should coat the beef!

instructions.title: Cook rice
instructions.instruction: Cook rice according to package directions.
instructions.tips: Fluffy rice is key!

instructions.title: Fry plantains
instructions.instruction: Heat remaining oil in pan over medium-high heat (about 350°F). Fry plantain slices in batches 2-3 minutes per side until golden and caramelized. Drain on paper towels.
instructions.tips: Plantains should be sweet and crispy!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: On each plate, arrange rice, black beans, shredded beef, and fried plantains in sections. Garnish with cilantro.
instructions.tips: The 'flag' presentation is traditional!

storage.freezer: Beef and beans freeze up to 3 months
storage.reheating: Microwave components separately

recipes.name: Tacos al Pastor
recipes.description: Marinated pork tacos with pineapple and cilantro-onion topping

equipment_substitutions.note: High heat for char.

instructions.title: Make marinade
instructions.instruction: Toast dried chilies in dry pan 30 seconds per side. Soak in hot water 15 minutes. Drain. In blender, combine soaked chilies, quartered onion, garlic, achiote paste, cumin, oregano, vinegar, pineapple juice, and salt. Blend until smooth.
instructions.tips: Smooth marinade coats meat evenly!

instructions.title: Marinate pork
instructions.instruction: Place pork slices in bowl. Pour marinade over, massage into meat. Marinate in fridge at least 2 hours or overnight.
instructions.tips: Overnight = maximum flavor!

instructions.title: Grill pork and pineapple
instructions.instruction: Heat grill or cast-iron pan to high heat. Remove pork from marinade, shaking off excess. Grill pork 3-4 minutes per side until charred and cooked through. Grill pineapple slices 2 minutes per side until caramelized. Chop pork and pineapple into small pieces.
instructions.tips: High heat creates char!

instructions.title: Warm tortillas
instructions.instruction: Heat tortillas on grill or in pan until warm and pliable, about 30 seconds per side.
instructions.tips: Stack and wrap to keep warm!

instructions.title: Assemble tacos
instructions.instruction: Fill each tortilla with chopped pork and pineapple. Top with diced onion and cilantro. Serve with lime wedges and salsa verde.
instructions.tips: Simple toppings let pork shine!

storage.fridge: Marinated pork stores 3 days; cooked pork stores 4 days
storage.freezer: Marinated pork freezes up to 3 months
storage.reheating: Pan-fry to crisp up edges

recipes.name: Lebanese Kibbeh
recipes.description: Bulgur wheat and ground meat croquettes with pine nuts and spices

instructions.title: Soak bulgur
instructions.instruction: Rinse bulgur in cold water. Soak in ice water 15 minutes. Drain well, squeeze out excess water.
instructions.tips: Cold water keeps bulgur from getting mushy!

instructions.title: Make filling
instructions.instruction: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in pan over medium heat. Cook diced onion 5 minutes. Add ground beef, cook 8 minutes until browned. Add 1 tsp allspice, 1/2 tsp cinnamon, 1/2 tsp cumin, salt, and pepper. Stir in pine nuts. Cool completely.
instructions.tips: Filling must be cold!

instructions.title: Make shell mixture
instructions.instruction: In food processor, combine soaked bulgur, ground lamb, grated onion, remaining allspice, cinnamon, cumin, salt, and pepper. Process until smooth paste forms, about 3 minutes. Add ice water gradually if mixture seems dry.
instructions.tips: Paste should be smooth and moldable!

instructions.title: Form kibbeh
instructions.instruction: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). With wet hands, take golf-ball sized portion of shell mixture. Make indentation with finger, creating a cup shape. Add 1 tbsp filling. Seal opening, shape into football. Repeat with remaining mixture.
instructions.tips: Keep hands wet to prevent sticking!

instructions.title: Bake kibbeh
instructions.instruction: Arrange kibbeh on oiled baking sheet. Brush with remaining olive oil. Bake 25-30 minutes until golden brown and cooked through.
instructions.tips: Can also deep fry for traditional version!

instructions.title: Make yogurt sauce
instructions.instruction: Mix yogurt with diced cucumber, pinch of salt, and drizzle of olive oil.
instructions.tips: Cool yogurt balances rich kibbeh!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Serve hot kibbeh with yogurt sauce and fresh salad.
instructions.tips: Best eaten fresh and hot!

storage.fridge: Raw kibbeh store 2 days; cooked store 4 days
storage.freezer: Raw kibbeh freeze beautifully up to 3 months
storage.reheating: Oven at 350°F for 15 minutes

recipes.name: Persian Ghormeh Sabzi
recipes.description: Herb stew with lamb, kidney beans, and dried limes

ingredients.name: leeks
ingredients.preparation: white and light green parts, chopped

instructions.title: Brown lamb
instructions.instruction: Season lamb with salt and pepper. Heat 2 tbsp oil in Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown lamb in batches, about 8 minutes total. Remove and set aside.
instructions.tips: Good browning = deep flavor!

instructions.title: Cook onions
instructions.instruction: In same pot, add onion. Cook 8 minutes until golden. Add garlic and turmeric, cook 1 minute.
instructions.tips: Turmeric gives beautiful color!

instructions.title: Cook herbs
instructions.instruction: Heat remaining oil in large pan over medium heat. Add chopped parsley, cilantro, fenugreek, and leeks. Cook 10-12 minutes, stirring frequently, until herbs are wilted and dark green. This step is crucial!
instructions.tips: Herbs must cook down - don't rush this!

instructions.title: Combine and braise
instructions.instruction: Add browned lamb back to pot with onions. Add cooked herbs, water, and dried limes. Bring to boil, reduce to low simmer. Cover and cook 90 minutes, stirring occasionally.
instructions.tips: Dried limes add unique sour note!

instructions.title: Add beans
instructions.instruction: Add kidney beans. Continue simmering uncovered 30 minutes until lamb is very tender and stew has thickened. Taste and adjust salt.
instructions.tips: Beans should be tender but not mushy!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Remove dried limes (or leave for presentation). Serve over Persian rice (tahdig) with yogurt on the side.
instructions.tips: Tastes even better the next day!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat, add splash of water if needed

recipes.name: Turkish Menemen
recipes.description: Scrambled eggs with tomatoes, peppers, and Turkish spices

instructions.title: Cook vegetables
instructions.instruction: Melt butter in large pan over medium heat. Add onion and green peppers, cook 6 minutes until softened.
instructions.tips: Don't brown - just soften!

instructions.title: Add tomatoes
instructions.instruction: Add garlic, cook 30 seconds. Add diced tomatoes and Aleppo pepper. Cook 6-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tomatoes break down and release their juices.
instructions.tips: Tomatoes should be saucy!

instructions.title: Add eggs
instructions.instruction: Crack eggs directly into pan. Season with salt and pepper. Gently stir eggs into tomato mixture, creating soft curds. Cook 3-4 minutes until eggs are just set but still creamy.
instructions.tips: Don't overcook - eggs should be soft!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Remove from heat. Sprinkle with feta (if using) and parsley. Serve immediately with crusty bread.
instructions.tips: Perfect Turkish breakfast!

storage.reheating: Not recommended

recipes.name: Moroccan Tagine
recipes.description: Slow-cooked chicken with preserved lemons, olives, and warm spices

equipment_substitutions.note: Use any heavy pot with lid.

instructions.title: Season chicken
instructions.instruction: Pat chicken dry. Season with salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, and cinnamon. Rub spices all over chicken.
instructions.tips: Coating chicken with spices builds flavor!

instructions.title: Brown chicken
instructions.instruction: Heat olive oil in tagine or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown chicken skin-side down 5 minutes until golden. Flip and brown other side 3 minutes. Remove and set aside.
instructions.tips: Don't skip browning - it adds flavor!

instructions.title: Cook aromatics
instructions.instruction: In same pot, add onion. Cook 5 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
instructions.tips: Scrape up brown bits from chicken!

instructions.title: Add saffron and stock
instructions.instruction: Steep saffron in 2 tbsp hot stock 5 minutes. Add saffron mixture and remaining stock to pot. Stir well.
instructions.tips: Steeping releases saffron's color and flavor!

instructions.title: Braise chicken
instructions.instruction: Return chicken to pot. Add half the cilantro and parsley. Bring to simmer, cover, reduce heat to low. Cook 45 minutes until chicken is tender.
instructions.tips: Low heat keeps chicken tender!

instructions.title: Add preserved lemons and olives
instructions.instruction: Add preserved lemon quarters and olives. Simmer uncovered 15 minutes until sauce thickens slightly.
instructions.tips: Preserved lemons are the signature flavor!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Garnish with remaining cilantro and parsley. Serve with couscous or flatbread.
instructions.tips: The sauce is incredible over couscous!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat

recipes.name: Iraqi Masgouf
recipes.description: Grilled carp with tamarind sauce and traditional Iraqi spices

equipment_substitutions.note: Broil at high temperature.

instructions.title: Prepare fish
instructions.instruction: Rinse butterflied fish, pat dry. Score flesh in diamond pattern. Season both sides with salt, pepper, cumin, turmeric, and paprika.
instructions.tips: Scoring helps spices penetrate!

instructions.title: Make marinade
instructions.instruction: Mix olive oil, garlic, lemon juice, tamarind paste, and half the parsley. Brush generously over fish, inside and out. Let marinate 15 minutes.
instructions.tips: Tamarind adds signature tangy flavor!

instructions.title: Prepare grill
instructions.instruction: Heat grill to medium-high heat. Oil grates well.
instructions.tips: Well-oiled grates prevent sticking!

instructions.title: Grill vegetables
instructions.instruction: Grill tomato halves and onion rounds 4-5 minutes per side until charred and softened. Set aside.
instructions.tips: Char adds smoky flavor!

instructions.title: Grill fish
instructions.instruction: Place fish skin-side down on grill. Grill 15-20 minutes without moving. Carefully flip, grill another 15-20 minutes until fish is cooked through and crispy.
instructions.tips: Traditional method cooks flesh-side first over open fire!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Transfer fish to large platter. Surround with grilled tomatoes and onions. Garnish with remaining parsley. Serve with flatbread and pickles.
instructions.tips: National dish of Iraq - serve family-style!

storage.reheating: Oven at 350°F for 10 minutes

recipes.name: Palestinian Musakhan
recipes.description: Roasted chicken with sumac, caramelized onions, and taboon bread

instructions.title: Season chicken
instructions.instruction: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Rub chicken pieces with 2 tbsp sumac, allspice, cumin, salt, and pepper. Drizzle with 2 tbsp olive oil. Place in baking dish.
instructions.tips: Sumac gives signature tangy flavor!

instructions.title: Roast chicken
instructions.instruction: Pour stock into baking dish. Roast chicken 45-50 minutes until golden and cooked through, basting occasionally.
instructions.tips: Basting keeps chicken moist!

instructions.title: Caramelize onions
instructions.instruction: While chicken roasts, heat remaining olive oil in large pan over medium heat. Add sliced onions, cook 25-30 minutes, stirring frequently, until deeply caramelized and golden brown. Stir in remaining sumac. Season with salt.
instructions.tips: Patience = sweet, jammy onions!

instructions.title: Prepare bread
instructions.instruction: Brush flatbreads with pan juices from roasted chicken. Arrange on large serving platter, overlapping slightly.
instructions.tips: Chicken juices soak into bread!

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.instruction: Spread caramelized onions over bread. Arrange chicken pieces on top. Drizzle with lemon juice and any remaining pan juices. Sprinkle with toasted pine nuts and parsley.
instructions.tips: Eat with your hands, family-style!

storage.fridge: 3 days in airtight container; store components separately
storage.freezer: Chicken and onions freeze up to 2 months
storage.reheating: Oven at 350°F for 15 minutes; assemble fresh

recipes.name: Syrian Fattet Hummus
recipes.description: Layered chickpea dish with tahini yogurt, crispy pita, and pine nuts

instructions.title: Crisp pita
instructions.instruction: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Arrange pita triangles on baking sheet. Bake 8-10 minutes until golden and crispy. Set aside.
instructions.tips: Watch closely - they burn quickly!

instructions.title: Make tahini yogurt sauce
instructions.instruction: In bowl, whisk together yogurt, tahini, garlic, lemon juice, cumin, and salt. Mixture should be thick but pourable. Add water if too thick.
instructions.tips: Smooth and creamy is the goal!

instructions.title: Warm chickpeas
instructions.instruction: Heat chickpeas with their liquid in pot over medium heat 5-6 minutes until warm. Drain, reserving liquid.
instructions.tips: Keep chickpeas warm for serving!

instructions.title: Toast pine nuts
instructions.instruction: Melt butter in small pan over medium heat. Add pine nuts, toast 3-4 minutes until golden, stirring constantly. Remove from heat.
instructions.tips: Pine nuts burn easily - watch them!

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.instruction: Arrange crispy pita pieces on serving platter. Top with warm chickpeas. Pour tahini yogurt sauce over everything. Drizzle with butter-pine nut mixture. Sprinkle with paprika and parsley.
instructions.tips: Assemble just before serving!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Serve immediately while pita is still crispy. Traditionally eaten with spoon.
instructions.tips: The contrast of textures is key!

storage.fridge: Components store 2 days separately; assemble fresh
storage.reheating: Warm chickpeas only; assemble fresh

recipes.name: Emirati Machboos
recipes.description: Spiced rice with lamb and dried limes

instructions.title: Brown lamb
instructions.instruction: Heat 2 tbsp ghee in Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Season lamb with salt and 1 tbsp baharat. Brown on all sides, about 8 minutes. Remove and set aside.
instructions.tips: Good browning = rich flavor!

instructions.title: Cook aromatics
instructions.instruction: In same pot, add remaining ghee and onions. Cook 8 minutes until golden. Add garlic, ginger, remaining baharat, turmeric. Cook 2 minutes.
instructions.tips: Baharat is the key spice blend!

instructions.title: Add tomatoes and spices
instructions.instruction: Add tomatoes, dried limes, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods. Cook 5 minutes until tomatoes break down.
instructions.tips: Dried limes add unique flavor!

instructions.title: Cook lamb
instructions.instruction: Return lamb to pot. Add hot stock. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cover and cook 60 minutes until lamb is tender.
instructions.tips: Lamb should be fall-apart tender!

instructions.title: Add rice
instructions.instruction: Remove lamb to plate. Add drained rice to pot, stir gently. Nestle lamb pieces on top of rice. Cover tightly, cook on low heat 20-25 minutes until rice is cooked and liquid absorbed.
instructions.tips: Don't lift lid while rice cooks!

instructions.title: Rest
instructions.instruction: Remove from heat. Let stand covered 10 minutes.
instructions.tips: Resting perfects the rice texture!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Fluff rice gently. Transfer to serving platter with lamb on top. Garnish with toasted almonds, raisins, and cilantro.
instructions.tips: A celebratory dish!

storage.reheating: Microwave 3-4 minutes or stovetop with splash of water

recipes.name: Yemeni Saltah
recipes.description: Spicy meat stew with fenugreek foam and flatbread

instructions.title: Prepare fenugreek foam
instructions.instruction: Drain soaked fenugreek seeds. Blend with 2 cups water until frothy and thick like meringue, about 5 minutes. Set aside in fridge.
instructions.tips: This foam is the signature of saltah!

instructions.title: Brown lamb
instructions.instruction: Heat oil in pot over high heat. Season lamb with salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, and turmeric. Brown 8 minutes. Remove and set aside.
instructions.tips: High heat for good browning!

instructions.title: Cook aromatics
instructions.instruction: In same pot, add onions. Cook 6 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
instructions.tips: Scrape up browned bits!

instructions.title: Braise lamb
instructions.instruction: Return lamb to pot. Add tomatoes and remaining 2 cups water. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cover and cook 75 minutes until lamb is very tender.
instructions.tips: Low and slow = tender lamb!

instructions.title: Add zhug and eggs
instructions.instruction: Stir in zhug. Pour beaten eggs over top, stirring gently to create ribbons. Cook 2 minutes until eggs are set.
instructions.tips: Eggs add richness!

instructions.title: Add fenugreek foam
instructions.instruction: Top stew with fenugreek foam. Gently fold in slightly - it should remain on top mostly. Cook 2 minutes.
instructions.tips: Don't overmix - foam should float!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Serve immediately in traditional stone bowls if available, or regular bowls. Garnish with cilantro. Serve with flatbread for dipping.
instructions.tips: The foam should be airy and light on top!

storage.fridge: 3 days; fenugreek foam best made fresh
storage.freezer: Stew freezes up to 2 months; make foam fresh
storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat; add fresh foam

recipes.name: Jordanian Mansaf
recipes.description: Lamb cooked in jameed yogurt sauce served over rice and flatbread

instructions.title: Cook lamb
instructions.instruction: Place lamb in large pot with onion, cardamom, bay leaves, turmeric, and salt. Cover with water. Bring to boil, skim foam. Reduce to simmer, cover. Cook 75 minutes until tender. Reserve 4 cups cooking liquid.
instructions.tips: Save that flavorful broth!

instructions.title: Prepare jameed sauce
instructions.instruction: If using dried jameed, reconstitute in warm water. Whisk jameed (or yogurt) until smooth. Slowly add 3 cups reserved lamb cooking liquid, whisking constantly. Heat gently over low heat, stirring constantly, 10 minutes. Do not boil or it will curdle.
instructions.tips: Low heat prevents curdling!

instructions.title: Cook rice
instructions.instruction: Melt butter in pot. Add drained rice, toast 2 minutes. Add remaining cup lamb broth and 2.5 cups water. Bring to boil, reduce to low. Cover and cook 18 minutes until tender.
instructions.tips: Fluffy rice is essential!

instructions.title: Finish lamb
instructions.instruction: Add cooked lamb to jameed sauce. Simmer gently 10 minutes to blend flavors.
instructions.tips: Gentle heat keeps sauce smooth!

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.instruction: Arrange flatbreads on large platter. Top with rice, creating a mound. Arrange lamb pieces on rice. Pour jameed sauce over everything. Sprinkle with toasted almonds, pine nuts, and parsley.
instructions.tips: Traditional presentation is key!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Serve family-style. Traditionally eaten with hands, using bread to scoop.
instructions.tips: National dish of Jordan!

storage.freezer: Lamb freezes up to 3 months; make sauce fresh
storage.reheating: Gentle heat for sauce; microwave rice and lamb

recipes.name: Ethiopian Doro Wat
recipes.description: Spicy chicken stew with berbere spice and hard-boiled eggs

ingredients.name: niter kibbeh
ingredients.preparation: Ethiopian spiced butter, or regular butter

instructions.title: Caramelize onions
instructions.instruction: Heat 2 tbsp niter kibbeh in large pot over medium heat. Add onions, cook 20-25 minutes, stirring frequently, until deep golden brown and caramelized. This step is crucial for authentic flavor!
instructions.tips: Patience here = incredible depth of flavor!

instructions.title: Add aromatics and spices
instructions.instruction: Add garlic, ginger, berbere, cardamom, and fenugreek. Cook 3 minutes, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
instructions.tips: Berbere is the soul of this dish!

instructions.title: Add tomato paste
instructions.instruction: Stir in tomato paste. Cook 2 minutes to deepen flavor.
instructions.tips: This adds richness and color!

instructions.title: Add chicken
instructions.instruction: Add chicken pieces to pot. Coat well with onion-spice mixture. Add remaining niter kibbeh, salt, and pepper. Cook 10 minutes, turning chicken occasionally.
instructions.tips: Coating chicken builds flavor layers!

instructions.title: Braise
instructions.instruction: Add chicken stock. Bring to boil, reduce to low simmer. Cover and cook 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
instructions.tips: Low simmer keeps chicken tender!

instructions.title: Add eggs
instructions.instruction: Score hard-boiled eggs with shallow cuts. Add to pot, simmer uncovered 15 minutes. Sauce should be thick and cling to chicken.
instructions.tips: Scoring lets eggs absorb flavor!

instructions.title: Finish
instructions.instruction: Stir in lemon juice. Taste and adjust salt. Serve with injera (Ethiopian flatbread) or rice.
instructions.tips: Traditionally eaten with hands using injera!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat, add splash of water if needed

recipes.name: Nigerian Jollof Rice
recipes.description: One-pot tomato rice with chicken and West African spices

instructions.title: Season and cook chicken
instructions.instruction: Season chicken with salt, curry powder, thyme, and half the chopped onion. Let sit 10 minutes. In pot, heat 2 tbsp oil. Brown chicken 10 minutes. Remove and set aside.
instructions.tips: Seasoned chicken = flavorful rice!

instructions.title: Make pepper blend
instructions.instruction: In blender, combine tomatoes, red bell peppers, scotch bonnet, remaining chopped onion, garlic, and ginger. Blend until smooth.
instructions.tips: Smooth blend is key to creamy sauce!

instructions.title: Cook pepper base
instructions.instruction: Heat remaining oil in same pot over medium-high heat. Add sliced onion, fry 3 minutes. Add tomato paste, fry 2 minutes. Pour in blended pepper mixture. Cook 25-30 minutes, stirring frequently, until oil rises to surface and sauce is thick and deep red.
instructions.tips: This frying stage is crucial - don't rush it!

instructions.title: Add stock and seasoning
instructions.instruction: Add chicken stock, bouillon cubes, bay leaves, and browned chicken. Stir well. Taste and adjust salt. Bring to boil.
instructions.tips: Bouillon adds umami depth!

instructions.title: Add rice
instructions.instruction: Stir in rice. Ensure rice is submerged in liquid. If needed, add a bit more stock. Bring to boil, reduce to lowest heat. Cover tightly with foil, then lid. Cook 30 minutes without lifting lid.
instructions.tips: Don't peek - trapping steam is essential!

instructions.title: Check and rest
instructions.instruction: After 30 minutes, check if rice is tender and liquid absorbed. If rice needs more cooking, add splash of water, cover, cook 5 more minutes. Remove from heat, let rest 10 minutes covered.
instructions.tips: Resting perfects the texture!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Fluff rice gently. Remove bay leaves. Serve hot with fried plantains and salad.
instructions.tips: The party rice of West Africa!

storage.reheating: Microwave with splash of water or stovetop

recipes.name: Senegalese Thieboudienne
recipes.description: Fish and rice with vegetables in tomato sauce

ingredients.name: grouper
ingredients.preparation: or other firm white fish, cut into steaks

ingredients.name: nokoss
ingredients.preparation: Senegalese spice paste (or use garlic-parsley paste)

instructions.title: Prepare nokoss (stuffing)
instructions.instruction: Mix half the parsley with half the garlic, salt, and pepper. Make slits in fish steaks, stuff with nokoss mixture. Set aside.
instructions.tips: Stuffing infuses fish with flavor!

instructions.title: Fry fish
instructions.instruction: Heat oil in large pot over medium-high heat. Fry fish steaks 3-4 minutes per side until golden. Remove carefully and set aside.
instructions.tips: Don't overcook - fish finishes cooking later!

instructions.title: Make tomato base
instructions.instruction: In same oil, add onions. Cook 5 minutes. Add tomato paste and remaining garlic. Cook 10 minutes, stirring frequently, until oil separates and paste darkens.
instructions.tips: Dark tomato paste = deep flavor!

instructions.title: Cook vegetables
instructions.instruction: Add fish stock, scotch bonnets (whole), cassava, and carrots. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook 15 minutes. Add eggplant and cabbage, cook 10 more minutes until vegetables are tender.
instructions.tips: Layer vegetables by cooking time!

instructions.title: Remove vegetables
instructions.instruction: Carefully remove all vegetables to a platter. Keep warm. Remove and discard scotch bonnets.
instructions.tips: Vegetables stay on the side!

instructions.title: Cook rice
instructions.instruction: Add rice to the pot with tomato broth. Stir once. Place fried fish on top of rice. Bring to boil, reduce to low heat. Cover tightly, cook 25-30 minutes until rice is tender and liquid absorbed.
instructions.tips: Rice absorbs all that flavorful broth!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Fluff rice. Serve on large platter with rice, fish on top, vegetables arranged around. Sprinkle with remaining parsley.
instructions.tips: National dish of Senegal!

storage.reheating: Microwave with splash of water

recipes.name: South African Bobotie
recipes.description: Curried mince with egg topping and dried fruit

instructions.title: Cook aromatics
instructions.instruction: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Melt butter in pan over medium heat. Cook onions 6 minutes until soft. Add garlic, curry powder, turmeric, and ginger. Cook 2 minutes.
instructions.tips: Curry spices need to bloom!

instructions.title: Cook meat
instructions.instruction: Add beef and lamb to pan. Cook 8 minutes, breaking up meat until browned. Season with salt and pepper.
instructions.tips: Break meat up well for even texture!

instructions.title: Add bread and fruit
instructions.instruction: Squeeze excess milk from soaked bread. Add bread, apricots, raisins, chutney, and lemon juice to meat. Mix well. Taste and adjust seasoning.
instructions.tips: Bread keeps it moist; fruit adds sweetness!

instructions.title: Transfer to baking dish
instructions.instruction: Spread meat mixture into greased 9x13 baking dish. Press down gently to create even layer.
instructions.tips: Smooth top helps egg topping set evenly!

instructions.title: Make egg topping
instructions.instruction: Beat eggs with remaining 1 cup milk and pinch of salt. Pour over meat mixture. Arrange bay leaves on top. Sprinkle with almonds.
instructions.tips: Egg creates beautiful golden top!

instructions.title: Bake
instructions.instruction: Bake 35-40 minutes until egg topping is set and golden. Let rest 10 minutes before serving.
instructions.tips: Don't overbake or topping gets rubbery!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Remove bay leaves. Serve with yellow rice, sambals, and chutney.
instructions.tips: Sweet and savory Cape Malay classic!

storage.reheating: Oven at 325°F for 20 minutes

recipes.name: Ghanaian Groundnut Soup
recipes.description: Peanut-based soup with chicken and vegetables

ingredients.name: scotch bonnet pepper
ingredients.preparation: whole or chopped for more heat

instructions.title: Season and brown chicken
instructions.instruction: Season chicken with salt and pepper. Heat oil in large pot over medium-high heat. Brown chicken 8 minutes. Remove and set aside.
instructions.tips: Browning adds depth!

instructions.title: Cook aromatics
instructions.instruction: In same pot, add onions. Cook 6 minutes. Add garlic, ginger, and tomato paste. Cook 3 minutes, stirring constantly.
instructions.tips: Cook tomato paste well to remove raw taste!

instructions.title: Add tomatoes
instructions.instruction: Add crushed tomatoes and scotch bonnet. Cook 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until sauce thickens.
instructions.tips: Leave scotch bonnet whole for less heat!

instructions.title: Add peanut butter
instructions.instruction: Mix peanut butter with 2 cups warm chicken stock until smooth. Add to pot, stirring well to combine. Add remaining stock and browned chicken.
instructions.tips: Mixing peanut butter first prevents lumps!

instructions.title: Simmer
instructions.instruction: Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cover and cook 30 minutes until chicken is tender.
instructions.tips: Gentle simmer keeps peanut butter smooth!

instructions.title: Add vegetables
instructions.instruction: Add eggplant and okra. Simmer uncovered 15 minutes until vegetables are tender. Taste and adjust salt.
instructions.tips: Okra thickens the soup naturally!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Remove scotch bonnet if left whole. Serve hot with fufu, rice, or boiled plantains.
instructions.tips: Rich, nutty, and comforting!

storage.reheating: Stovetop over low heat, add water if too thick

recipes.name: Kenyan Nyama Choma
recipes.description: Grilled marinated goat or beef with kachumbari salad

equipment_substitutions.note: Broil at high heat.

ingredients.name: goat meat
ingredients.preparation: bone-in pieces (or use beef short ribs)

instructions.title: Marinate meat
instructions.instruction: Mix lemon juice, garlic, ginger, oil, salt, and pepper. Rub all over meat pieces. Marinate at least 2 hours or overnight in fridge.
instructions.tips: Longer marination = more tender meat!

instructions.title: Prepare grill
instructions.instruction: Prepare charcoal grill for medium-high heat. Oil grates well.
instructions.tips: Charcoal adds authentic smoky flavor!

instructions.title: Grill meat
instructions.instruction: Grill meat pieces 20-25 minutes, turning every 5 minutes, until charred outside and cooked through. Baste with remaining marinade during grilling.
instructions.tips: Goat takes longer than beef!

instructions.title: Make kachumbari
instructions.instruction: While meat grills, mix tomatoes, red onion, cilantro, lime juice, chili, and pinch of salt. Let sit to meld flavors.
instructions.tips: Fresh, bright salad cuts richness!

instructions.title: Rest meat
instructions.instruction: Remove meat from grill, let rest 5 minutes.
instructions.tips: Resting keeps meat juicy!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Serve grilled meat with kachumbari salad, ugali (cornmeal porridge), and extra lemon wedges.
instructions.tips: Kenya's favorite BBQ!

storage.reheating: Grill or oven to re-crisp exterior

recipes.name: Tunisian Shakshuka
recipes.description: Eggs poached in spicy tomato and pepper sauce

instructions.title: Cook aromatics
instructions.instruction: Heat olive oil in large pan over medium heat. Add onion and bell peppers, cook 8 minutes until soft.
instructions.tips: Soft vegetables = sweet base!

instructions.title: Add spices
instructions.instruction: Add garlic, cumin, paprika, and harissa. Cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
instructions.tips: Harissa adds signature heat!

instructions.title: Add tomatoes
instructions.instruction: Pour in crushed tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer 10-12 minutes until sauce thickens.
instructions.tips: Sauce should be thick enough to hold eggs!

instructions.title: Add eggs
instructions.instruction: Make 8 wells in sauce. Crack an egg into each well. Cover pan, cook 5-7 minutes until whites are set but yolks are still runny.
instructions.tips: Don't overcook - runny yolks are best!

instructions.title: Finish and serve
instructions.instruction: Sprinkle with feta and parsley. Serve immediately with crusty bread for dipping.
instructions.tips: Perfect for breakfast, lunch, or dinner!

storage.fridge: Sauce stores 3 days; add fresh eggs when serving
storage.reheating: Reheat sauce, add fresh eggs

recipes.name: Cambodian Amok Trey
recipes.description: Steamed fish curry in banana leaves with coconut and lemongrass

equipment_substitutions.note: Works but banana leaves add aroma.

instructions.title: Make curry paste
instructions.instruction: In blender, combine lemongrass, galangal, 4 kaffir lime leaves, turmeric, chilies, shallots, and garlic. Blend until smooth paste forms. Add oil if needed.
instructions.tips: Smooth paste is essential!

instructions.title: Make curry base
instructions.instruction: In bowl, mix curry paste with 300ml coconut cream, fish sauce, and palm sugar. Reserve remaining 100ml coconut cream. Whisk until smooth.
instructions.tips: Thick, rich curry base!

instructions.title: Add fish and egg
instructions.instruction: Gently fold fish chunks into curry mixture. Fold in beaten eggs and half the basil leaves.
instructions.tips: Gentle folding keeps fish intact!

instructions.title: Prepare banana leaves
instructions.instruction: Soften banana leaves by briefly passing over flame or dipping in hot water. Line 4 small bowls or ramekins with banana leaves, shiny side up.
instructions.tips: Softening prevents tearing!

instructions.title: Fill and top
instructions.instruction: Divide fish curry mixture among banana leaf cups. Top each with 2 tbsp reserved coconut cream, shredded remaining kaffir lime leaves, and remaining basil.
instructions.tips: Coconut cream creates beautiful top layer!

instructions.title: Steam
instructions.instruction: Set up steamer with shredded cabbage in bottom (prevents sticking). Place filled banana cups on top. Steam 20-25 minutes until custard is set and fish is cooked.
instructions.tips: Don't over-steam or custard gets watery!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Serve hot in banana leaves with jasmine rice.
instructions.tips: Cambodia's national dish!

storage.reheating: Steam 10 minutes

recipes.name: Jamaican Jerk Chicken
recipes.description: Spicy marinated chicken with allspice and scotch bonnet

equipment_substitutions.note: Bake at 425°F.

instructions.title: Make jerk marinade
instructions.instruction: In blender, combine green onions, scotch bonnets, garlic, ginger, allspice, thyme, cinnamon, nutmeg, brown sugar, soy sauce, lime juice, oil, salt, and pepper. Blend until smooth paste.
instructions.tips: Allspice is the key jerk flavor!

instructions.title: Marinate chicken
instructions.instruction: Score chicken pieces deeply with knife. Rub jerk marinade all over, getting into cuts. Marinate at least 4 hours or overnight in fridge.
instructions.tips: Overnight = maximum flavor penetration!

instructions.title: Prepare grill
instructions.instruction: Prepare grill for medium heat (350-400°F). For authentic flavor, use pimento wood chips if available.
instructions.tips: Traditional jerk is cooked over pimento wood!

instructions.title: Grill chicken
instructions.instruction: Oil grill grates. Grill chicken pieces 35-40 minutes, turning every 8-10 minutes, until charred outside and cooked through (165°F internal temp).
instructions.tips: Some char is good - that's authentic jerk!

instructions.title: Rest and serve
instructions.instruction: Let chicken rest 5 minutes. Serve with rice and peas, fried plantains, and festival (sweet fried dumplings).
instructions.tips: The perfect Caribbean BBQ!

storage.fridge: Marinated chicken stores 2 days; cooked stores 4 days
storage.freezer: Marinated chicken freezes up to 3 months
storage.reheating: Grill or oven to re-crisp skin

recipes.name: Australian Meat Pie
recipes.description: Savory beef pie with flaky pastry and rich gravy

instructions.title: Cook vegetables
instructions.instruction: Melt butter in large pan over medium heat. Cook onion and carrots 6 minutes. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
instructions.tips: Soften vegetables first!

instructions.title: Brown beef
instructions.instruction: Add beef, cook 8 minutes, breaking up until browned. Season with salt, pepper, and thyme.
instructions.tips: Good browning = rich flavor!

instructions.title: Add flour and liquids
instructions.instruction: Sprinkle flour over beef, stir 2 minutes. Add tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, beef stock, and bay leaf. Stir well.
instructions.tips: Flour thickens the gravy!

instructions.title: Simmer
instructions.instruction: Bring to boil, reduce to simmer. Cook uncovered 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and gravy-like. Remove bay leaf. Let cool completely.
instructions.tips: Must be cool before adding to pastry!

instructions.title: Assemble pie
instructions.instruction: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line pie dish with one sheet puff pastry. Fill with cooled beef mixture. Top with second pastry sheet. Trim edges, crimp to seal. Cut slits in top for steam.
instructions.tips: Good seal prevents leaking!

instructions.title: Bake
instructions.instruction: Brush top with beaten egg. Bake 35-40 minutes until pastry is golden brown and puffed.
instructions.tips: Egg wash gives beautiful shine!

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.instruction: Let rest 10 minutes before slicing. Serve with tomato sauce (ketchup) and mushy peas.
instructions.tips: Australian classic comfort food!

storage.freezer: Unbaked pies freeze up to 3 months
storage.reheating: Oven at 350°F for 15-20 minutes
